Site.name: Human Aging Simulators
Site.sloganTagline: More good years of life, lived to the fullest.
Site.tagline: An open system for humanity to extend, together, the good years of life.

Hero.eyebrow: A common cause for all of humanity · Open science · Come and join
Hero.title: More years to enjoy life — unhurried, healthy, with the people we love — and an open system where every person on the planet can contribute.
Hero.subtitle: Human Aging Simulators (HAS) integrates financial donations, clinical data, discoveries and technology into one open platform. Every contribution — from a person, a researcher or a donor — adds up to the same goal: extending the good years of human life, together.
Hero.metricGoalDesc: Extend the healthy lifespan of the human species
Hero.metricInputsDesc: Financial donation, personal data, discoveries and systems
Hero.metricOpenDesc: MIT/Apache code · CC-BY data and content
Hero.metricGovernanceDesc: Dynamic consent, bioethics committee, external audit

Mission.title: A longer, healthier life is within reach. We just have to build it together.
Mission.p1: Today information about longevity is fragmented: private labs, closed papers, datasets that don't talk to each other, devices that don't share metrics, countries that don't even compare their populations. That's lost time our species can't afford to keep losing.
Mission.p2: Human Aging Simulators (HAS) proposes the opposite: a single, open, reproducible system where anyone — wherever they live, whatever they have — can contribute financial donations, data, discoveries or technology. And where every contribution adds up to the same goal: making it normal, not exceptional, to live 90, 100 or 110 years in good health.
Mission.p3: We don't compete with medicine or pharma. We build the shared infrastructure all of humanity can use to understand, measure and modify aging. And we build it together, in public, step by step.
Mission.visionStatement: Build scientific models capable of simulating and understanding the human body to accelerate biomedical discoveries.

Vision.imageAlt: Illustration of a bright horizon with figures from different generations — children, adults and elders — sharing the landscape, a symbol of a long life lived in good company.
Vision.quote: Picture yourself reaching 100 with a clear mind, strong muscles, your people close, and all the time in the world to enjoy the journey.
Vision.body: We are not trying to prolong old age. We are trying to extend the good years of life — the ones lived without rush, in good health, fully present, with the people we love. Every dollar, every data point and every idea humanity contributes here moves us closer to that horizon. If you want to get there, you are already part of the cause.

HowWeDoIt.title: Three complementary ways to feed the system.
HowWeDoIt.subtitle: Anyone can contribute through any of the three. Combined, they accelerate everything.

money.title: Financial donations
money.description: Cover infrastructure, sequencing, storage, scientific validation and grants for researchers. Every dollar shows up in monthly public reports.

data.title: Donating clinical and life data
data.description: Citizens who share — anonymously, encrypted, revocable at will — their genome, biomarkers, clinical records, vitals, habits and environment. Without this data there is no real science.

knowledge.title: Sharing discoveries and technology
knowledge.description: Researchers, scientists, engineers and journalists contributing datasets, software systems, papers, models, clinical findings or outreach. Everything integrates into one common system.

HowWeDoIt.footnote: The system accepts datasets, software systems, research, devices and anything that adds up to the goal. What already exists isn't reinvented — it's integrated.

System.title: Human Longevity Operating System: a digital biological twin of humanity.
System.subtitle: A layered architecture that takes real data, computes biological age per organ, models causality and simulates interventions safely.

data.label: Layer 1
data.title: Human data
data.description: Genome, epigenome, transcriptome, proteome, metabolome, microbiota, biomarkers, medical imaging, wearables, clinical history, diet, environment and behavior.

biological.label: Layer 2
biological.title: Biological age per organ
biological.description: Tissue-level biological clocks: brain, immune, muscle, liver, heart, kidney and endocrine. Not a single age — the functional age of each system.

causal.label: Layer 3
causal.title: Causal model
causal.description: Bayesian networks, causal models and graph neural networks to identify which mechanisms actually drive aging, not just what correlates with it.

intervention.label: Layer 4
intervention.title: Intervention simulator
intervention.description: Virtually test senolytics, partial reprogramming, mitochondrial therapies, somatic editing and geroprotectors before touching a real person.

safety.label: Layer 5
safety.title: Safety and bioethics
safety.description: Continuous risk assessment: cancer, fibrosis, immune failure, loss of cell identity, neurological damage. What isn't safe doesn't move forward.

longevity.label: Layer 6
longevity.title: Healthy longevity prediction
longevity.description: Not just living longer — reaching 90, 100 or 110 years with cognition, muscle strength, immunity and cellular repair intact.

node.title: Longevity Node — the system endpoint
node.intro: A wearable + patch + app + integrated lab, connected to your personal medical record and the central model.
items: Daily capture of vitals, sleep, glucose, heart rate and HRV, temperature and oxygen.
Periodic blood, epigenetic, proteomic, metabolomic and immune profiles.
Scheduled medical imaging: MRI, DEXA, ultrasound, cardiovascular and brain imaging.
Connected clinical record with dynamic consent, encrypted data, identity-from-biology separation.
Personal longevity AI returning plain-language reports and recommendations.

Disciplines.title: Ten scientific disciplines on a single platform.
Disciplines.subtitle: Aging cannot be understood from one science alone. Human Aging Simulators (HAS) spans from DNA to behavior and bioethics.

genomics.title: Human genomics
genomics.description: Variants associated with longevity, DNA repair, telomeres, somatic mutations and cancer risk.

epigenomics.title: Epigenomics
epigenomics.description: DNA methylation, histone modifications, epigenetic clocks and Yamanaka-style partial reprogramming.

transcriptomics.title: Transcriptomics
transcriptomics.description: Which genes turn on or off with age, with tissue-level differences: brain, muscle, liver, skin, blood.

proteomics.title: Proteomics
proteomics.description: Protein changes, inflammation, cell signaling and per-organ proteomic clocks.

metabolomics.title: Metabolomics
metabolomics.description: NAD+, glucose, lipids, amino acids, mitochondria, insulin sensitivity and oxidative damage.

cellAging.title: Cellular aging biology
cellAging.description: Cellular senescence, autophagy, proteostasis, mitochondrial dysfunction and stem-cell exhaustion.

immunology.title: Immunology
immunology.description: Inflammaging, immunosenescence and immune surveillance against cancer.

systemsAi.title: Systems biology + AI
systemsAi.description: Multi-omic models, gene regulatory networks, biological digital twins and intervention simulation.

regenerative.title: Regenerative medicine
regenerative.description: Stem cells, gene therapies, partial reprogramming and tissue regeneration.

ethics.title: Bioethics and safety
ethics.description: Tumor risk, restricted germline editing, biological inequality, consent and genetic control.

Roles.title: What each person gets back for contributing.
Roles.subtitle: Every contribution is acknowledged, documented and returned as concrete value.

donor.title: For those who contribute financial donations
donor.intro: Donors fund the infrastructure that holds everything up: sequencing, compute, validation, grants and operations.
benefits: Monthly reports on scientific, technical and financial progress.
Official recognition as principal donors of the human longevity cause.
Full traceability: see how every dollar was spent and what result it produced.
Early access to relevant findings before they are published openly.

citizen.title: For those who share their data
citizen.intro: Anyone can upload clinical, genomic, biometric and lifestyle data — encrypted, anonymous, revocable — and receive personalized science back.
benefits: Personal reports on clinical, genomic and statistical progress.
Per-organ biological age and your personal aging speed.
Nutrition, exercise and prevention recommendations based on your data.
Free access to every tool the system builds for your healthy longevity.

collaborator.title: For researchers, scientists and journalists
collaborator.intro: Any professional who contributes datasets, systems, papers, clinical findings or outreach is integrated and credited as an author.
benefits: Official recognition as a contributor to the discoveries you helped produce.
Co-authorship on papers published in bioRxiv with open peer review.
Access to datasets, public API, MCP and project compute.
Public visibility in monthly reports and the collaborators wall.

Privacy.title: Stricter than any social network. More transparent than any pharma company.
Privacy.subtitle: A person's genome also reveals information about their family. Governance matters as much as the science.
items: Dynamic informed consent: change your mind whenever you want.
Pseudonymized, end-to-end encrypted data, separated from your identity.
No selling to insurers, employers or third parties for commercial use.
Right to leave the study and erase your data at any time.
Independent bioethics committee with external audit and public reporting.
Germline editing forbidden. No proprietary therapies. No corporate capture.

Roadmap.title: From landing to global open scientific infrastructure.
Roadmap.subtitle: We are in Phase 1. Each phase is validated locally before committing cloud resources.

phase1.label: Phase 1 — Active
phase1.title: Landing & early traction
phase1.description: Public page, financial transparency, first donations, citizens and collaborators onboarded. Real-demand validation.

phase2.label: Phase 2 — Next
phase2.title: Data pipeline & synthetic population
phase2.description: Global synthetic cohorts based on public aggregates, first dataset on Zenodo, first paper on bioRxiv.

phase3.label: Phase 3
phase3.title: Digital biological twin
phase3.description: Per-organ biological age, intervention simulators, first version of the Longevity Node and personalized reports for citizens.

phase4.label: Phase 4
phase4.title: Public API + MCP + global community
phase4.description: Open API and MCP server so any researcher or external LLM can plug into the system with traceable citations.

openScience.title: Open science
openScience.description: MIT/Apache code, CC-BY data and content. Everything is released so the community can audit, replicate and extend it.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: Every decision, every dollar and every finding is documented and published. Monthly open reports — no opacity.

ethicalAi.title: Ethical AI
ethicalAi.description: Auditable models, explainable decisions, no manipulation or discrimination. Independent bioethics committee with external audit.

humanityFirst.title: Humanity first
humanityFirst.description: Every decision puts human benefit before commercial gain. No proprietary patents, no corporate capture, no lock-in.

reproducible.title: Reproducible research
reproducible.description: Every method, dataset and result is published so any team can replicate it step by step. Science that can't be replicated isn't science.

anonymizedData.title: Anonymized data
anonymizedData.description: End-to-end encryption, identity separated from biology, no sale to insurers or third parties. Your data stays yours.

globalAccess.title: Global access
globalAccess.description: Multilingual, free and available to any country, hospital or person on the planet. Longevity must not be a privilege.

notMedical.title: Not a medical device
notMedical.description: Human Aging Simulators (HAS) is educational and research-oriented. It does not diagnose, treat or replace clinical advice. Labeled visibly, always.

Status.title: We are on day one. In public. And that's good news.
Status.p1: Human Aging Simulators (HAS) is at concept stage: solo founder, self-funded, no external funding yet. That means anyone arriving now — you, today — joins while there is still everything to build, and your name can stay in the foundations forever.
Status.p2: If you believe an open, global, multidisciplinary system to extend the good years of human life is something the world needs, there are three immediate ways to push it: donate, share your data or collaborate. Any one of them already counts.
Status.metricTransparencyValue: Monthly public reports from day one

FinalCta.title: More good years of life are possible. We just need many people to say yes — today.
FinalCta.subtitle: Any of the three paths is already a real step. The three together make it happen sooner, for more people.

Footer.tagline: Open system integrating financial donations, data and discoveries so humanity can extend healthy lifespan.
Footer.rights: Content under CC-BY · Code under permissive licenses (MIT/Apache).

Disclaimer.notMedicalDevice: Human Aging Simulators (HAS) is not a medical device. The simulations, reports and projections are educational and research models. They do not constitute diagnosis, treatment or clinical advice.

hero.eyebrow: Donors — Pushing the human cause of longevity
hero.title: Your financial donations fund the infrastructure all of humanity will use to live better, for longer.
hero.subtitle: You are not funding a company. You are funding an open layer that any country, hospital, university or person will be able to use to understand and slow down aging. Every dollar is a promise: more good years for someone, somewhere.

what.title: What every dollar is spent on
items: Genomic and epigenomic sequencing of pilot cohorts.
Compute to train multi-omic models and biological digital twins.
Encrypted, audited storage of sensitive data.
Grants for researchers contributing time to the project.
Legal, bioethical and external-audit operations.
Keeping all code and data open to the community.

items.title: Monthly reports
items.description: Each month you get a closed report on scientific, technical and financial progress. What was discovered, what was spent, what comes next.

items.title: Official recognition
items.description: Principal donors are listed on the public wall of honor and officially cited as the people who pushed the human cause of longevity forward.

items.title: Total traceability
items.description: Every dollar tagged: which workstream it funded, which result it produced, what share of the monthly budget it represented.

items.title: Early access
items.description: Relevant scientific findings reach you before their open publication so you can read, discuss or redirect your contributions.

items.title: Voice in priorities
items.description: Recurring donors take part in quarterly surveys on research priorities and roadmap.

items.title: No corporate capture
items.description: You don't fund proprietary patents or therapies. Your financial donations do not become private property — they become shared infrastructure.

tiers.title: Suggested levels
tiers.subtitle: Indicative levels. The goal is sustainability, not luxury.

items.name: Ally
items.description: Monthly report, mention in yearly public acknowledgments.

items.name: Patron
items.description: Everything above + early access to findings and public patrons wall.

items.name: Principal donor
items.description: Everything above + official recognition as principal donor of the human cause of longevity and voice in priorities.

items.a: Not yet. We are evaluating legal structure (non-profit, fund, or fiscal host via OpenCollective). We will announce when this is settled.
Yes, within the first 30 days, no questions asked. After that it depends on whether the budget has been executed.

items.q: Does my name have to be public?
items.a: Your call. You can donate fully anonymously, or appear on the public wall with name, organization or pseudonym.

ctaBlock.title: First donations are the ones that prove this is worth pushing.
ctaBlock.subtitle: We are still wiring up Stripe and OpenCollective. In the meantime, write to us to coordinate your contribution.

form.title: Make a donation
form.subtitle: Pick an amount and a frequency. Payment is securely processed by Stripe — we never store your card details.

accountCta.title: Want to track the impact of your donation?
accountCta.subtitle: Create your donor account to see, month by month, how every dollar is used and download your receipts.

pause.title: We are setting up the proper legal structure to accept donations
pause.subtitle: In the meantime, there are other equally important ways to contribute to the project.
pause.legal: This pause is temporary. Soon, backed by a formal nonprofit entity (Open Source Collective or a registered Mexican civil association), we will reopen donations with full transparency and tax deductibility where it applies. Thank you for your patience.
pause.citizensTitle: I am a person who wants to share my data
pause.collaboratorsTitle: I am a researcher, scientist or journalist

hero.eyebrow: Citizens — Your body, your story, your control
hero.title: What you share today can give millions of people more good years tomorrow — starting with you.
hero.subtitle: You share clinical, genomic, biometric and lifestyle data — encrypted, anonymous, revocable whenever you want. In return you get a full read of how your body is aging and personalized tools to take better care of yourself, for longer.

what.title: What data we will ask for (always with your consent)
items: Whole genome (one time in your life).
Epigenome and biomarkers every 6-12 months.
Clinical data: diagnoses, medications, surgeries, family history.
Scheduled medical imaging: MRI, DEXA, ultrasound when relevant.
Wearables: heart rate and HRV, sleep, glucose, activity, temperature, oxygen.
Habits and environment: diet, exercise, stress, sun exposure, pollution, general location.

items.title: Per-organ biological age report
items.description: Not a single age. Your brain, immune, muscular, hepatic, cardiovascular, renal and endocrine ages with their confidence intervals.

items.title: Your personal aging speed
items.description: How much faster or slower you age relative to your chronological age, and which life events accelerate or slow it down.

items.title: Personal clinical and genomic reports
items.description: Clinical, genomic, proteomic, metabolic and statistical updates in plain language, with a complete picture of how your life is evolving.

items.title: Nutrition recommendations
items.description: Dietary guidance personalized to your metabolism, microbiota, genetics and current biomarkers.

items.title: Exercise recommendations
items.description: Type, intensity and frequency tuned to your per-organ biological age, not your birthday.

items.title: Access to every tool
items.description: As the system grows, you get free access to every new prevention, simulation, screening and self-care tool released.

privacy.title: Your privacy is stricter than any social network
items: Dynamic informed consent: you can change your mind whenever you want.
End-to-end encryption and separation between your identity and your biological data.
No selling to insurers, employers or third parties for commercial use.
Right to delete all your data from the system at any time.
Independent bioethics committee with public external audit.
Your genetic data also reveals information about your family — this is treated with the weight it deserves.

items.q: How much does it cost to participate?
items.a: Zero for you. Tests, devices and infrastructure are covered by donations. If you want to co-fund your package, we can also enable that.
The full form opens in Phase 2. Meanwhile you can join the waitlist to be among the first people in once it's ready.
No. Human Aging Simulators (HAS) is not a medical device. The reports are educational and research-oriented. Any relevant finding should be validated with your physician.
Full exit with data deletion at any time, no questions. It's a right, not a concession.

ctaBlock.title: Your body is the only real source of evidence.
ctaBlock.subtitle: Without citizens sharing data there is no science. Join the waitlist to come in as soon as the form is ready.

hero.title: Your work joins the system, stays attributed to your name, and pushes the whole species toward a better life.
hero.subtitle: Human Aging Simulators (HAS) integrates datasets, systems, papers, clinical findings, models and outreach into one shared platform. Every contribution is cited, versioned and officially recognized. What you discover here doesn't get lost or locked away — it stays, and becomes shared heritage.

who.title: Who can collaborate
items: Researchers in longevity, genomics, epigenetics, proteomics and systems biology.
Physicians, cell biologists, immunologists and regenerative-medicine specialists.
Data, ML/AI, software and biomedical-device engineers.
Science journalists, communicators and translators who help carry the cause.
Bioethicists, lawyers and privacy/consent specialists.
Students and volunteers with time and technical discipline.

what.title: What you can contribute
items: Your own datasets (synthetic or derived from public aggregates).
Software systems: pipelines, models, MCP servers, devices, dashboards.
Papers, preprints, scientific validations and replications.
Clinical findings, publishable cases and systematic reviews.
Outreach: articles, podcasts, video, translations, educational content.
Bioethical, legal or privacy advisory.

items.title: Official recognition
items.description: Any discovery or breakthrough you contribute to stays attributed to your name, in the system and in publications.

items.title: Co-authorship on bioRxiv
items.description: You co-sign papers and preprints published with open peer review. Citable, reproducible.

items.title: Access to datasets and API
items.description: Synthetic datasets on Zenodo, public API and MCP server to plug your LLMs (Claude, GPT) directly into the system.

items.title: Project compute
items.description: Access — by availability and priority — to donor-funded compute for training models and validating hypotheses.

items.title: Visibility in reports
items.description: Your name and contribution appear in public monthly reports and the collaborators wall.

items.title: No corporate capture
items.description: You don't sign IP transfers to a company. Your work stays under permissive licenses (MIT/Apache, CC-BY) — visible forever.

CollaboratorsPage.disciplinesNote: The 10 disciplines we integrate are described on the home page — from genomics to bioethics.

items.q: Is there funding for my time?
items.a: Not yet at scale. As donations come in, minimal grants will open for collaborators with sustained dedication. Initial priority is seriousness and recognition, not salary.
Yes, as long as you have rights to release it under a compatible license (MIT/Apache for code, CC-BY for data and content).
Yes. We seek framework agreements with universities, hospitals and research centers. Write to us to open one.
Everything contributed to Human Aging Simulators (HAS) is released under permissive licenses. There are no proprietary patents inside the project.

ctaBlock.title: The system needs people who add knowledge, not hoard it.
ctaBlock.subtitle: If you work in any related discipline and feel your contribution adds up, write to us. We start with a conversation.

Simulator.metaDescription: Explore the HAS physiological simulation engine: adjust a meal and watch blood glucose respond live, driven by the Bergman model.
Simulator.title: A digital human that ages
Simulator.subtitle: HAS models the body as coupled physiological systems that evolve over time. Here you can see the first of them in action: glucose metabolism.

intro.heading: What the engine is
intro.body: The HAS engine simulates an individual as a set of physiological systems —metabolic, cardiovascular, endocrine, immune…— that interact step by step. Each system is grounded in published scientific models, and it is deterministic and reproducible: the same configuration and the same seed always produce the same trajectory. The goal is to explore how habits and interventions shape aging across decades.

demo.title: Demo: the glucose response to a meal
demo.intro: Adjust a meal's carbohydrates and the individual's age. The curve shows how blood glucose rises and falls over the next four hours. All the computation runs in your browser.
demo.legend: The green band marks the healthy post-meal range; the red line, the hyperglycemia threshold.
demo.chartAlt: Chart of blood glucose over the four hours following a meal.

status.healthy: Glucose peaks at {minute} min and stays within the healthy range.
status.elevated: Glucose peaks at {minute} min, above the ideal range.
status.hyper: Glucose crosses the hyperglycemia threshold at {minute} min.

model.heading: How the model works
model.body: Glucose metabolism uses the Bergman minimal model: four coupled differential equations describing plasma glucose, insulin, its action on tissues, and the intestinal absorption of carbohydrates. A meal loads the gut; glucose appears gradually in the blood; the pancreas secretes insulin, which clears it. With age, insulin sensitivity declines, so the same plate produces a higher and longer-lasting peak in an older person.

Simulator.disclaimer: This simulation is educational and exploratory. HAS is not a medical device or a diagnostic tool, and it does not replace the advice of a health professional.

cta.heading: The simulator learns from real people
cta.body: The more people contribute their health data anonymously, the more faithful the digital human becomes. That is the citizen science behind HAS.

PrivacyPage.metaDescription: How we collect, store, and share your data at HAS. Executive summary, your rights, and a link to the full legal text.
PrivacyPage.title: Your data, your control
PrivacyPage.subtitle: HAS only collects the data you choose to upload to your vault. Donors see aggregated statistics with k-anonymity ≥ 10. You never lose control.
PrivacyPage.draftNotice: Draft version (0.1) pending legal review per jurisdiction. For the lawyer-audited version, see the public repository.

tldr.title: Executive summary
tldr.collect: We only collect the data you upload to your vault (clinical, biometric, psychological, lifestyle) plus minimal account data (email, name, language).
tldr.why: We use it to give you personal analysis tools and for open longevity research in aggregated, anonymized form.
tldr.access: Only you access your individual data. The operator (super_admin) accesses only with justification and it is logged in audit.
tldr.donors: Donors see only aggregated statistics with k-anonymity ≥ 10. They NEVER access individual data.
tldr.collaborators: Collaborators (researchers) see only aggregates, or pseudonymized data if you gave specific consent per study.
tldr.encryption: AES-256 encryption at rest (AWS KMS), TLS 1.3 in transit.
tldr.minAge: Minimum age 18. We do not collect data from minors in V1.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.intro: Under GDPR, HIPAA, LGPD and LFPDPPP you have the right to:
rights.access: Access: obtain a full copy of your data in FHIR format.
rights.erasure: Erasure (right to be forgotten): permanent deletion of your data.
rights.portability: Portability: export your data in structured format.
rights.objection: Objection: withdraw consent at any time.
rights.accounting: Accounting of disclosures: know who accessed and when. SLA: 15 days.
rights.dsrLink: You can exercise all these rights directly from the <link>admin console</link> under My vault → DSR.

contact.title: How to contact us
contact.body: For any privacy question or DSR request outside the app, email us at <mail>privacy@haslife.org</mail> or use the <form>contact form</form>.

full.title: Full legal version
full.body: The version audited per jurisdiction (LFPDPPP/HIPAA/GDPR/LGPD) lives in the public repository: <link>privacy-policy.es.md</link>. This summary is optimized for reading; the complete document is the binding legal reference.

LicensesPage.metaDescription: Licenses for HAS code, documentation, datasets, and key dependencies. Open source end to end.
LicensesPage.title: Open source end to end
LicensesPage.subtitle: HAS code, documentation, and datasets are open by design. Here are the specific licenses for each layer and our main dependencies.

code.title: Code
code.body: All HAS code is licensed under MIT. See the <link>LICENSE file</link> in the public repository for the complete terms.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.body: Documentation (SDD specs, ADRs, guides) is under <link>Creative Commons CC-BY 4.0</link>. You can copy, modify, and redistribute it with attribution.

data.title: Datasets
data.body: Aggregated datasets we publish on Zenodo or with DOI follow <link>Creative Commons CC-BY 4.0</link>. Individual personal data is never redistributed.

deps.title: Main dependencies
deps.intro: We thank the open source ecosystem. Our core dependencies:
deps.note: Non-exhaustive list. For the full inventory, see the pnpm licenses list command in the repository.
